activities_projects.title: Poem Understanding Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the meaning of the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the situation faced by the poet
Write why the poet could not travel both roads
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the central situation in the poem

activities_projects.title: Stanza Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze each stanza of the poem
steps: Divide the poem into four stanzas
Write the main idea of each stanza
Discuss your answers in class

activities_projects.title: Imagery Identification
activities_projects.objective: Identify imagery used in the poem
steps: Find lines that describe nature
Write how the poet describes the forest
Explain how imagery helps visualize the poem

activities_projects.title: Choice and Consequence Chart
steps: Draw two columns named 'Choice' and 'Result'
Write the poet’s choice in the first column
Write possible results of that choice in the second column
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the relationship between choices and outcomes

activities_projects.title: Figure of Speech Identification
activities_projects.objective: Identify poetic devices in the poem
steps: Find examples of metaphor in the poem
Write what the two roads symbolize
Explain the meaning of the metaphor

activities_projects.title: Compare the Two Roads
steps: Write the description of the first road
Write the description of the second road
Explain how both roads are actually similar

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion
steps: Discuss why the poet chose the less travelled road
Share opinions with classmates
Write the group’s conclusion

activities_projects.title: Personal Reflection Writing
activities_projects.objective: Relate the poem to personal life
steps: Think about a decision you made in your life
Write how that decision affected you
Compare it with the poet’s decision

activities_projects.title: Poetic Tone Activity
activities_projects.objective: Identify the tone of the poem
steps: Read the last stanza carefully
Write the tone of the poet (thoughtful, reflective, etc.)
Explain why the poet says it with a sigh

activities_projects.title: Alternative Ending Activity
steps: Imagine the poet took the other road
Write 5-6 sentences describing what might happen
Share your ideas in class
